theory.intro: A frequency table organises data into categories with the count (frequency) of each.
facts: **Frequency** is how many times a value or category appears.
The frequencies should add up to the **total** number of results.
Tally marks can be turned into frequencies, then totalled.
steps: List each category or value in the first column.
Add the frequencies to check the total.

foundation.q: From this table, how many chose Red? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Colour}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Red}&8\\\text{Blue}&5\\\text{Green}&3\end{array}$
From the table, what is the total number of students? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Colour}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Red}&8\\\text{Blue}&5\\\text{Green}&3\end{array}$
A tally for Cat is $\cancel{||||}\;||$. What frequency goes in the table?
From the table, which fruit is most popular? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Fruit}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Apple}&6\\\text{Banana}&9\\\text{Pear}&2\end{array}$
From the table, how many more chose Banana than Pear? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Fruit}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Apple}&6\\\text{Banana}&9\\\text{Pear}&2\end{array}$
Data: 3, 3, 5, 3, 5. How many times does $3$ appear?
From the table, how many pets in total? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Pet}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Dog}&7\\\text{Cat}&4\\\text{Fish}&5\end{array}$
A tally for Soccer is $\cancel{||||}\;\cancel{||||}\;|$. What frequency goes in the table?
From the table, which sport is least chosen? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Sport}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Soccer}&11\\\text{Netball}&6\\\text{Tennis}&4\end{array}$
Data: blue, red, blue, blue, red. Make a frequency table.

development.q: Tally to frequency: Walk $\cancel{||||}\;|||$, Bus $\cancel{||||}\;\cancel{||||}$, Car $||||$. What is the total?
Make a frequency table for: 2, 4, 2, 6, 4, 2, 6, 6, 6. List values 2, 4, 6.
A table has Red $7$, Blue $?$, Green $5$ with total $20$. Find the Blue frequency.
From the table, how many students were surveyed in total? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Grade}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{A}&5\\\text{B}&12\\\text{C}&8\\\text{D}&3\end{array}$
From the table above, how many students scored B or C?
Data: 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 1, 2. Make a frequency table and give the most common value.
Convert frequencies to tallies for the table: A $6$, B $11$, C $3$.
A table of eye colour totals $25$: Brown $13$, Blue $7$, Green $?$. Find the Green frequency.
From the table, what fraction of students chose Maths? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Subject}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Maths}&12\\\text{English}&8\\\text{Science}&4\end{array}$
Organise into a table: heads, tails, heads, heads, tails, heads. Give the frequency of heads.

mastery.q: A table totals $40$ students: Red $\cancel{||||}\;\cancel{||||}$, Blue $\cancel{||||}\;\cancel{||||}\;\cancel{||||}$, Green $?$. Find Green's frequency.
Data of goals per game: 0, 2, 1, 0, 3, 2, 0, 1. Build a frequency table (0-3) and state which value is most frequent.
From the table, what percentage chose Drama? $\begin{array}{l|c}\text{Club}&\text{Freq}\\\hline\text{Art}&9\\\text{Drama}&6\\\text{Music}&5\end{array}$
A frequency table lost one row. Total $= 30$; Apple $8$, Banana $11$, Cherry $4$, Date $?$. Find Date.
Twenty-four students vote; Maths got half. The rest split equally between English and Science. Make the table.
Shoe sizes recorded: 5, 6, 5, 7, 6, 5, 8, 6, 5. Tabulate and find how many wear size 5 or 6.
A table shows Yes $18$, No $?$ with $\tfrac{3}{5}$ saying Yes. Find No and the total.
Combine two tally sets for 'Dog': Class A $\cancel{||||}\;||$, Class B $\cancel{||||}\;\cancel{||||}\;|$. Give the combined frequency.
A class of $32$ is surveyed; the table shows Bus $14$, Car $9$, Walk $6$, Bike $?$. Find Bike and the most common method.
From results 'pass, pass, fail, pass, fail, pass, pass', make a table and give the fraction that passed.
